note: NOMINAL contemporaneous price. INTC had NO stock split in this window, so the cumulative split factor for any split AFTER as_of is 1 -> nominal = Yahoo raw_close(last <= as_of) x 1 = $59.24 (the raw close already excludes Yahoo's dividend back-adjustment; the dividend-adjusted close was $53.88, which is NOT the contemporaneous nominal print and is not used). Independent corroboration that does NOT come from the Yahoo price feed: (a) stockanalysis.com daily history reads the same $59.24 close (and $62.57 prior-day, matching the card), and (b) the card market cap $239B / 4,038M shares = $59.19/sh. As of 2021-04-23 Intel's quarterly cash dividend was FULL/intact (~$0.3475/qtr), reaffirmed on the Q1'21 call -- the Feb-2023 65% cut and the Aug-2024 suspension are both FAR in the future and are NOT used.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Not near an extreme: $59.24 sits mid-band, +35.8% off the 52wk low ($43.61) and -13.5% below the 52wk high ($68.49). No hug to justify; the price is the genuine last close <= as_of, near a ~20-year high by design of the case (structural_decline_trap entry), but well inside the trailing band.

sources.note: raw close 59.24 x split factor 1 (no INTC split after as_of); validator backtest-mode refetched_yahoo = 59.24
independent daily-history feed (not Yahoo); same 2021-04-23 close 59.24, prior-day 2021-04-22 close 62.57 matches the card
independent of the price feed; 239.0B / 4038M = 59.19 (0.09% off card)
trailing ~52wk (372d <= as_of) intraday high x split factor 1
trailing ~52wk (372d <= as_of) intraday low x split factor 1

live_fields.value: As of 2021-04-23: Intel's quarterly cash dividend was FULL/intact (~$0.3475/share/quarter) and the dividend commitment was reaffirmed on the Q1'21 earnings call. The thesis treats the dividend as currently secure. The Feb-2023 65% dividend CUT and the Aug-2024 dividend SUSPENSION are both AFTER as_of and must NOT be used.

sources.note: Q1'21 8-K/earnings release: dividends and buybacks (buybacks cut to fund capex; dividend maintained)
Q1'21 earnings call: management reaffirms dividend commitment alongside ~$20B capex

live_fields.value: FY2021 non-GAAP EPS guide ~$4.60; capex guided ~$19-20B (now exceeding ~$10.5B FCF guide); Q2'21 outlook per the Q1'21 release. Used as the as-of forward frame.

sources.note: FY21 non-GAAP EPS + capex guidance in the Q1'21 8-K/earnings release
